419429.name: region
419429.datasetName: Political regimes - OWID based on V-Dem (v12) and Lührmann et al. (2018)

source.name: OWID based on V-Dem (v12) and Lührmann et al. (2018)
source.dataPublishedBy: Our World in Data, Bastian Herre
source.dataPublisherSource: Data comes from the Varieties of Democracy project (v12), sometimes using Lührmann et al's (2018) Regimes of the World classification, and own expansions and refinements.
source.additionalInfo:
This dataset provides information on political regimes, using data from the Varieties of Democracy project (v11.1), and the Regimes of the World classification by Lührmann et al. (2018).

We expand the countries and years covered, and refine the coding of the Regimes of the World classification. You can read a detailed description of the data in this post: https://ourworldindata.org/regimes-of-the-world-data

You can download the code and complete dataset, including supplementary variables, from GitHub: https://github.com/owid/notebooks/tree/main/BastianHerre/political_regimes

445675.name: democracy_eiu
445675.description:
The variable captures the extent to which citizens can choose their political leaders in free and fair elections, enjoy civil liberties, prefer democracy over other political systems, can and do participate in politics, and have a functioning government that acts on their behalf.

It ranges from 0 to 10 (most democratic).

It matches the variable DemocracyindexEIU in gapminder, with a few missing countries for 2020 and all for 2021 added.

source.name: Economist Intelligence Unit (2022) via gapminder
source.dataPublishedBy: Economist Intelligence Unit. 2022. Democracy Index 2021: The China challenge.
source.additionalInfo:
This dataset provides information on political regimes, using data from the Economist Intelligence Unit's Democracy Index (2022).

You can download the code and complete dataset, including supplementary variables, from GitHub: https://github.com/owid/notebooks/tree/main/BastianHerre/democracy
